content.body: This document exists to separate the core control concepts agents use when authoring App Behavior corpuses. Use it when deciding whether a value belongs in `mode`, `artifactRole`, `specProfile`, `depthTarget`, or `facet`.

content.title: Control Concepts
body: `mode` means product behavior state, such as `current`, `desired`, `ideal`, or `intermediate`.
`artifactRole` means the kind of object or artifact, such as `feature`, `requirement`, `mapping`, or `pending-decision`.
`specProfile` means why the package exists, such as refactor preservation, new feature design, UX audit, or minute-detail specification.
`depthTarget` means how detailed the package must be.
`facet` means which UX detail categories are included, omitted, pending, or out of scope.
Do not use `mapping`, `mixed`, `manifest`, or `generated-review` as UX modes. Those are artifact roles.

content.title: Artifact Roles
body: `manifest` controls the package profile, modes, depth, facets, omissions, and completion standard.
`feature` describes a broad product area with multiple surfaces, journeys, or subfeatures.
`subfeature` describes a coherent UX surface or workflow inside a feature.
`journey` describes a user goal and context across one or more flows.
`flow` describes a coherent path with start, end, alternate paths, blocked paths, and recovery paths.
`subflow` describes a stable behavioral unit inside a flow.
`requirement` describes one coherent governing App Behavior concept.
`observable-scenario` describes a user-observable state or situation such as loading, empty, error, invalid, dirty, stale, auth, restore, or recovery.
`acceptance-criterion` describes proof expectations for a requirement, scenario, or subflow.
`mapping` describes how current UX relates to desired UX.
`pending-decision` describes an unresolved UX decision that must remain visible.
`generated-review` describes generated Markdown or other non-governing review output.
`mixed` describes a collection containing multiple artifact roles.

content.title: Facet Library
body: `features` is required when a package needs broad product-area context. Omit it for small changes inside an existing feature.
`subfeatures` is required when a feature contains distinct UX surfaces or workflows. Omit it when the feature is too small to split.
`journeys` is required when user goals and context materially shape behavior. Omit it only for small, low-risk specs with existing journey coverage.
`flows` is required when user paths, alternate paths, blocked paths, or recovery paths need definition.
`subflows` is required when a flow has multiple stable behavioral units. Omit it when a single flow-level requirement is enough.
`requirements` is required for implementation-grade App Behavior truth.

`observableScenarios` captures user-observable runtime states. It is the canonical schema field and facet name for loading, empty, error, invalid, dirty, stale, auth, restore, success, and recovery scenarios.
`validation` captures rules, invalid states, blocking behavior, messaging needs, and recovery from invalid input. It must not define form implementation internals.
`recovery` captures how users recover from errors, invalid states, empty results, destructive choices, or dead ends.
`emptyLoadingError` captures major non-normal states that affect user understanding or available actions.
`copy` captures exact labels, messages, and content only when wording is behaviorally meaningful or explicitly governing.
`layoutSemantics` captures order, hierarchy, placement, responsive meaning, and progressive disclosure when they affect comprehension or behavior. It must not become pixel-level styling.
`accessibility` captures keyboard, focus, screen-reader, reduced-motion, and assistive behavior when relevant.
`permissions` captures auth, role, entitlement, and access behavior.
`persistence` captures what user-visible context is saved, restored, or intentionally not retained.
`navigation` captures URL, back/forward, return, refresh, modal dismissal, and cross-surface continuity behavior.
`edgeCases` captures meaningful edge cases, not every possible input permutation.
`acceptanceCriteria` captures proof expectations.
`currentDesiredMappings` captures the relationship between existing and target behavior.
`pendingDecisions` captures unresolved UX decisions and must not be hidden in notes.
